Value: 219","CreationDate":"2025-12-12","SUBJECT":"EDUCATION, WALES","CATEGORY":"EDUCATION, WALES","SUMMARY":"These Regulations apply to local authorities and governing bodies that provide food and drink on a school day to registered pupils at maintained primary schools, whether the food and drink is provided on school premises or not. For the purposes of these Regulations, “maintained primary school” means a maintained school that provides primary education (whether or not it also provides other kinds of education). “Maintained school” means a community, foundation or voluntary school, a community special school or a pupil referral unit in Wales or a maintained nursery school as defined in regulation 2(1). Where a maintained primary school also provides secondary education, these Regulations will apply only to food and drink provided to registered pupils in year 6 and below.","":""}

content: unless it falls within sub-paragraph (3). (2) (a) bacon; (b) burger consisting of a mixture of meat and other ingredients;  (c) canned or tinned meat;  (d) chorizo;  (e) corned meat;  (f) gammon;  (g) ham;  (h) meatballs;  (i) pastrami;  (j) pâté containing meat or liver of any mammalian or bird species;  (k) salami, including pepperoni;  (l) sausage consisting of a mixture of meat and other ingredients;  (m) a food that contains one of the following as an ingredient whether or not the food also contains any other ingredient—  (i) mechanically separated meat;  (ii) the carpus, the heart, the muscles of the head (other than masseters), the tarsus, the tail or the tongue of any mammalian or bird species recognised as fit for human consumption;  (n) any other shaped or formed meat product consisting of a mixture of meat and other ingredients and not falling within paragraphs (a) to (m).  (3) Food falling within sub-paragraph (2)(b), (h) or (n) is not a specified meat product if it is— (a) provided as part of a school lunch, and  (b) made using a mixture of meat and other ingredients—  (i) on school premises;  (ii) on premises owned or occupied by a local authority;  (iii) where the local authority or governing body of a maintained primary school have a contract with a third party to provide school lunches, on premises owned or occupied by that third party.  2. In this Schedule— “ any other shaped or formed meat product ” (“ unrhyw gynnyrch arall o gig wedi ei siapio neu ei ffurfio ”) does not include a sliced cut of meat; “ ham ” (“ ham ”) includes jambon de Paris, prosciutto, serrano ham and similar products; “ sausage ” (“ selsigen ”) includes chipolata, corn dog, frankfurter, hot dog, sausage meat and similar products. Regulation 3 SCHEDULE 2 School breakfast in maintained primary schools 1. Food and drink from each of the food and drink categories must be available on each school day. 2. —(1) Food and drink other than food and drink from the food and drink categories must not be provided. (2) But plain water (still or carbonated) may be provided. 3. A registered pupil provided with school breakfast must be given the option to choose one item from each of the food and drink categories. 4. Any plain milk provided must be— (a) whole milk or semi-skimmed milk where the drink is provided to a registered pupil in a nursery class, and  (b) semi-skimmed milk, 1% fat milk or skimmed milk where the drink is provided to a registered pupil in a reception year, year 1 or year 2 or to a junior pupil.  5. No salt is to be added to food, or to be made available to add to food, after the cooking process is complete. 6. In this Schedule— “ food and drink categories ” (“ categorïau bwyd a diod ”) means the food and drink categories listed in Table 1; “ reduced fat spreads ” (“ taeniadau â llai o fraster ”) means spreads made from unsaturated vegetable oil, including olive, rapeseed or sunflower oil. Table 1 Food and Drink Categories Plain milk and plain plant-based drinks Cereals – not coated or flavoured with chocolate, cocoa powder or sugar or any combination of those ingredients Fruit and vegetables Breads (at least 50% wholegrain) and reduced fat spreads Regulation 4 SCHEDULE 3 Food and drink provided as part of a school lunch in maintained primary schools Interpretation 1. In this Schedule, “ portion ” means an amount of a particular food provided to a registered pupil as part of a school lunch. Vegetables 2. —(1) At least two portions of vegetables must be provided each day. (2) At least six different vegetables must be provided each week. (3) In calculating the number of different vegetables provided under sub-paragraph (2), different vegetables provided as a single portion count as one vegetable. Fruit 3. —(1) At least one portion of f
